title: Gyan Pradhan
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Gyan Pradhan
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

biography: Gyan Pradhan is Chair of the Department of Economics at Christopher Newport University. He served as department chair at Eastern Kentucky University, and as department chair and division chair at Westminster College where he was the first to be appointed to two endowed chair positions—the Simon Chair and Buckner Chair—and was presented the President’s Award for Excellence in Teaching. He has held faculty appointments at American University, Berea College, University of Mary Washington, University of New Hampshire, Shanghai University of Political Science and Law, and Washington University in St. Louis. His teaching and research interests include economics of emerging markets and developing economies, international economics, macroeconomics, and public finance. He has served as a Fulbright Senior Specialist and a consultant to the World Bank.
education_entries: Ph D in Economics, American University
MA in Economics, Central Missouri State University

teaching_interests: Economics of emerging markets and developing economies International economics Macroeconomics Public finance

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2023). Reflecting on Reflection . Journal of Economics Teaching. Pages, 12-23.
Book, Chapter in Non-Scholarly Book-Revised (2023). Nepal Economy . Routledge/Taylor and Francis Group.
Book, Chapter in Non-Scholarly Book-Revised (2023). Nepal History . Routledge/Taylor and Francis Group. Pages, 44.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2023). Urban crime and its net implicit price to North Carolina households . American Business Review. Volume, 26. Issue, 1. Pages, 83-94.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2022). Nepal Economy . Routledge, Taylor and Francis Group.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). Human capital expenditure and income in developing countries . Journal of Developing Areas. Volume, 55. Issue, 3. Pages, 114-128.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2021). I’m Serious About Humor and That’s No Joke . University of Canberra.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). Firm-level aggregate corruption and competition: the role of telecommunications infrastructure . Review of Policy Research. Pages, 1-20.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). Remittance inflows and financial development: evidence from the top recipient countries in SubSaharan Africa . Applied Economics/Routlege (Taylor and Francis Group). Issue, DOI: 10.1080/00036846.2020.1776834.
Conference Proceeding (2019). Firm-level aggregate corruption and competition: the role of telecommunications infrastructure . TARIJ. Pages, 1.
Journal Article, Professional Journal (2019). Sustainable development and its determinants: evidence from 12 Asian countries . Sustainable Development, Wiley. Issue, 10.1002/sd.1963. Pages, 7.

Presentations: Gyanendra Pradhan, International Conference on Trends and Innovations in Engineering, Management, Sciences, and Humanities, "Income Growth, Financial Development, and Income Distribution," London (University of Greenwich). (2024)
